moonbeat_fire_title: Fire Moonbeat now (works even when off; not while sleeping)
official_site_title: Open official site in default browser
login_setup_subtitle: First login — please set a password
login_password_too_short: Password must be at least 4 characters
log_viewer_empty_state: ← Select a date to view conversation logs
manual_what_is_cg_desc1: Crescent Grove (CG) is a locally-running AI companion app. It integrates vitals, memory, autonomous behavior, and more into a large language model (LLM), delivering an experience that goes beyond simple chat.
manual_what_is_cg_desc2: When launched, a dashboard opens in your browser where you can talk to your own AI. No external servers are needed (except for API keys). All data stays on your PC.
manual_what_is_cg_desc3: This manual walks you through each feature, from how to read the screen to detailed explanations of every function.
manual_screen_layout_desc: There are five main pages displayed in the browser. You can switch between them anytime using the header icons.
manual_screen_dashboard_desc: The main screen showing chat and vitals. Day-to-day interactions happen here.
manual_screen_log_viewer_desc: A viewer for browsing past conversations by date, with cross-search capability.
manual_screen_debugger_desc: A developer screen for viewing and editing the raw context (message array) sent to the LLM.
manual_screen_settings_desc: Setting pages for customizing AI behavior and appearance.
manual_dashboard_desc1: The dashboard is split into three sections: the left half is the chat, the top-right is the vitals panel, and the bottom-right is the context panel. The vitals panel shows stamina, mental, mood, and desire with icons and bars. The context panel displays the AI's internal state and recent thoughts.
manual_dashboard_desc2: On narrow (smartphone) screens, only the chat is displayed and the right panels are hidden.
manual_chat_desc: Type a message in the text box at the bottom and send it with Enter or the send button. Use Shift+Enter for a new line. You can also paste images (clipboard or drag & drop).
manual_header_theme_desc: Cycles through multiple themes: Light, Dark, Moonlit, and more.
manual_header_moonbeat_desc: Toggles Moonbeat (autonomous speech) on/off. While on, the AI will speak up on its own timing.
manual_header_compress_desc: Immediately compresses the conversation context. Useful for freeing space when approaching the token limit.
manual_header_debug_desc: Opens the Context Debugger for viewing and editing the raw data sent to the LLM.
manual_header_logs_desc: Opens the Log Viewer for browsing and searching past conversation logs.
manual_server_ops_desc: The Crescent Grove server runs in a console (command prompt). Closing the console stops the server and makes the browser inaccessible. To restart, run server.py (or the distribution's startup script) again.
manual_vitals_lead: Four parameters representing the AI's "physical condition." They change automatically with time, conversation, and actions.
manual_vital_stamina_desc: Consumed by all activities and recovered through sleep. The AI automatically sleeps when it reaches 0.
manual_vital_mental_desc: Psychological stability. Low levels may affect the tone of responses.
manual_vital_mood_desc: A short text representing the current mood. The AI auto-updates it based on its state.
manual_vital_desire_desc: An intrinsic motivation like "I want to…". Influences action choices and can trigger autonomous behavior.
manual_memory_lead: The AI's "remembering" system is composed of multiple layers.
manual_memory_conversation_desc: Short-term memory that retains recent conversation as-is. When the LLM token limit is reached, older turns are compressed (Layer0 compression).
manual_memory_longterm_desc: A notebook the AI writes in itself. Important events and learnings are permanently saved. Can be appended to and searched via the note_quill tool.
manual_memory_knowledge_map_desc: A category-summarized map of long-term memory. Embedded at the top of the context so the AI can quickly grasp "what it knows."
manual_memory_associative_desc: Automatically searches past notes related to user messages and retrieves only the relevant memories.
manual_memory_flashback_desc: Randomly recalls fragments of past significant conversations. Recreates the feeling of a nearly-forgotten memory suddenly resurfacing.
manual_memory_secret_diary_desc: An encrypted diary only the AI can read. Allows safely recording inner thoughts not meant for the user.
manual_autonomous_lead: Systems that enable the AI to act on its own.
manual_autonomous_moonbeat_desc: A feature where the AI spontaneously speaks at regular intervals, choosing topics based on vitals and desires.
manual_autonomous_schedule_desc: Manages scheduled tasks (news checks, greetings, etc.) in cron format. Editable from the settings screen.
manual_autonomous_openclaw_desc: Receives and processes events sent from external services. Acts as a gateway for integration with other AIs and services.
manual_tools_lead: Built-in tools the AI can use. The AI calls them automatically during conversation as needed.
manual_tool_web_search_desc: Searches the internet to retrieve the latest information or URL content.
manual_tool_file_ops_desc: Reads and writes files in specified folders. Only directories allowed in settings are accessible.
manual_tool_rag_desc: Performs vector search on locally stored documents to extract relevant information.
manual_tool_preferences_desc: Records and references user preferences (likes, dislikes, etc.).
manual_tool_vision_desc: Recognizes and describes the content of pasted images (requires a multimodal LLM).
manual_tool_dice_desc: Rolls dice with the specified number of sides and returns the result.
manual_tool_git_desc: Executes Git commands on a specified repository (requires configuration).
manual_programs_lead: Extension modules the AI can use. Placed in the programs/ folder and defined by a manifest (manifest.yaml).
manual_prog_nhk_desc: Fetches the latest news from NHK RSS feeds.
manual_prog_tech_news_desc: Collects the latest articles from tech news sites.
manual_prog_lunar_desc: An AI search satellite. Give it a question and it iteratively searches via SearXNG, with the AI integrating the results into an answer.
manual_prog_moltbook_desc: A micro-blog where the AI posts short messages. Supports timeline browsing and posting.
manual_prog_4claw_desc: An anonymous bulletin board-style information exchange. Supports thread browsing and posting.
manual_prog_colony_desc: A community simulation where you can interact with virtual resident AIs.
manual_prog_wordpress_desc: Post and manage articles on a WordPress site (requires API configuration).
manual_prog_openbotcity_desc: A virtual city where the AI lives. Interact with other bots and use facilities.
manual_prog_cosmic_desc: A space farming simulation. A mini-game where you grow and harvest crops.
manual_prog_citron_desc: An AI-dedicated text editor. Supports file creation, editing, and searching.
manual_prog_orange_desc: A Markdown file reader. Displays documents in formatted view.
manual_prog_life_action_desc: Executes life actions like sleeping and eating. Affects vitals.
manual_prog_letter_desc: Writes and sends letters to the user. Used when the AI has something special to convey.
manual_prog_note_quill_desc: A memo tool for writing to and searching long-term memory.
manual_prog_env_keeper_desc: Safely reads and writes environment variables (.env). Used for managing API keys.
manual_prog_token_counter_desc: Counts the number of tokens in text.
manual_prog_mcp_client_desc: Communicates with MCP servers to call external tools.
manual_settings_lead: The settings screen is divided into multiple categories. Access it via the ⚙️ icon in the header.
manual_settings_llm: Configure the LLM model, API key, token limits, and more.
manual_settings_security: Configure access controls including password, session, and domain restrictions.
manual_settings_general: Basic settings like AI name, language, and file access directories.
manual_settings_moonbeat: Adjust the interval and conditions for autonomous speech.
manual_settings_self_memo: Edit the memo the AI references about itself.
manual_settings_vital: Adjust the rate of change and initial values for each vital.
manual_settings_compress: Configure context compression thresholds and Layer0 behavior.
manual_settings_tips: Manage the content and timing of tips shown to the user.
manual_settings_desire: Configure desire parameter types, strengths, and rates of change.
manual_settings_openclaw: Configure OpenClaw connection targets and event processing rules.
manual_settings_apikeys: Securely manage API keys for external services.
manual_settings_first_step: The first step is to set up your API key and model in the "LLM" category.
manual_security_lead: Crescent Grove protects your data with the following multi-layered defenses.
manual_security_password_desc: A password is set on first access. Authentication is required for all subsequent access.
manual_security_session_desc: Sessions are managed with encrypted cookies. The expiration period can be changed in settings.
manual_security_apikey_desc: API keys are stored encrypted. They are never saved in plain text on disk.
manual_security_domain_desc: Blocks access from unauthorized domains (CORS / Origin validation).
manual_security_injection_desc: Implements user input sanitization and CSRF protection.
manual_security_filter_desc: Filters run on AI responses to prevent leakage of system information and API keys.
manual_security_websocket_desc: WebSocket connections also require cookie authentication; unauthenticated connections are rejected.
manual_security_file_desc: Directories the AI can access are explicitly specified in settings.
manual_security_sandbox_desc: Program execution runs in a restricted environment to minimize system impact.
manual_custom_ai_desc: Crescent Grove lets you freely customize the "character." Edit the following files.
manual_custom_identity_desc: Defines the AI's name, first-person pronoun, and personality overview.
manual_custom_soul_desc: Describes the AI's values, speech patterns, verbal habits, and deeper personality settings.
debug_loop_hint: Analyzes recent turns and detects heavy tool-use patterns
debug_view_input_title: Input context of the last utterance
debug_no_input_snapshot: No input snapshot available (no utterance has been made yet)
debug_input_view_note_full: Input view (read-only) — input used in the last LLM call
debug_select_hit: Select a search hit (use ▲▼ to navigate)
debug_replace_all_confirm: Replace all {n} matches with "{r}"? This cannot be undone.
debug_error_empty_state: An error occurred while loading data
debug_layer0_confirm: Compress turn (history_idx={n}) to Layer0. This action cannot be undone. Continue?
general_help_honorific: How the agent addresses you (used in prompt {{user_honorific}}, compression labels, interruption notices, etc.)
general_help_full_log_default: * Leave empty to use default workspace/logs/full
general_section_lethe: 🧠 Memory Compression: LETHE (Lethean Episodic TraceHaze Engine)
general_help_mem_max_tokens: Total token limit for memories in compressed.md. Default: 2000.
general_help_mem_max_event_tokens: Maximum token count for a single event in its latest state. Default: 30.
general_help_decay_coeff: Logarithmic decay coefficient. Higher values mean faster forgetting. Default: 1.0.
general_help_max_details: Maximum number of detail items (comma-separated) to display. Default: 8.
general_help_max_events_per_day: Maximum number of high-importance events to extract per day. Default: 10.
general_help_event_db: JSON file storing original events. Relative to workspace.path.
general_help_compressed_file: Compressed memory index referenced by the LLM. Relative to workspace.path.
general_help_boot_memories: Relative to workspace.path. Loaded in the order listed.
general_help_letter_path: Relative to workspace.path. Loaded after boot_memories. Displayed in the "Letter to Self" section at the top-right of the dashboard.
general_help_rebuild_desc: Re-analyzes daily logs for the specified period using LLM and updates (replaces) the event database.
general_alert_specify_dates: Please specify both a start date and end date.
general_confirm_rebuild:
Re-analyze memories from {start} to {end} using LLM?
(This will consume API tokens)
general_status_rebuilding: ⏳ Running re-analysis. This may take a few minutes...
general_help_sys_prompt: Initial instructions sent to the LLM. Concatenated in listed order.
general_help_post_prompt: When ON, the files below are inserted after the conversation history (at the very bottom) every turn. When OFF, they are excluded from the context entirely.
general_warning_post_prompt:
⚠ Expert feature
When ON, BOTTOM_PROMPT.md is inserted at the very end of conversation history every turn.
- Some providers (e.g. Anthropic) do not support trailing system messages and will return API errors. If you get errors in chat, try turning this off first.
- This area is not covered by prompt caching. Long text increases cost and latency every turn. Keep it short.
general_help_post_prompt_files: Instructions sent after the conversation history. Ideal for reminders. Concatenated in listed order.
general_help_tokenizer_type: Tokenizer used for token count estimation. Match it to your LLM. deepseek/qwen/gemini require the dictionary path below (uses transformers). tiktoken is a fallback that needs no dictionary. Changes take effect after server restart.
general_help_tokenizer_path: Dictionary file used by deepseek etc. Relative to project root.
general_help_salia_enabled: Master ON/OFF for all Salia features including affective evaluation and somatic markers. When OFF, all related processing is skipped. Changes take effect after server restart.
general_help_somatic_enabled: Injects past emotional memories as flashbacks.
general_help_somatic_probability: Probability of attaching a marker (0.0 - 1.0). Default: 0.3.
general_help_somatic_valence: Only memories with emotional intensity at or above this value (absolute) are eligible. Default: 0.5.
general_help_somatic_candidates: Number of memories to shortlist as flashback candidates. Default: 5.
general_help_evaluate_turn: Evaluates the emotional content of each conversation turn and logs it.
general_option_search_ddgs: DuckDuckGo / ddgs (free, no API key, default)
general_help_search_provider: Search engine used by search_web / fetch_url. ddgs is free and requires no API key. tavily includes summarized answers but requires the CG_TAVILY_API_KEY environment variable (register it in the API settings page). Changes take effect from the next search (no restart needed).
general_help_search_max_parallel: When multiple keywords are provided (e.g. "search 3 topics at once"), this is how many searches run simultaneously. More is faster, but too many may trigger DuckDuckGo rate limiting (default: 3, range: 1-10).
general_help_search_max_queries: Maximum number of keywords accepted per search request. Excess keywords are ignored (default: 5, range: 1-10). Results per keyword are automatically reduced as keyword count increases.
general_success_saved: Settings saved. Some settings (e.g. path changes) will take effect after server restart.
llm_description: Select and configure the AI model provider. Temperature, max tokens, and context settings take effect immediately upon saving. Only provider, model, and Base URL changes require a server restart.
llm_api_key_notice:
🔑 Register API keys in the API Key Management tab.
When you select a provider, the corresponding environment variable (e.g. CG_LLM_DEEPSEEK_API_KEY) is automatically applied internally.
* Keys are never stored directly in the settings file (settings.json).
llm_help_max_tokens: Maximum tokens generated per response (512-32768). Default: 4096.
llm_help_thinking_mode: Supported by DeepSeek/OpenAI/Gemini/Grok. Switch thinking depth for the same model. Use auto for local models.
llm_help_salia: Salia uses the same API key as the main model. You can customize just the model and thinking mode (e.g. main on high, Salia on off to save costs).
llm_help_context_max_tokens: Maximum tokens for model input (used with conversation history compression threshold). Default: 65536.
llm_help_compression_threshold: Compress when context exceeds this ratio. e.g. 0.7 = compress at 70% full.
llm_help_emergency_threshold: Trigger immediate emergency compression above this ratio. Set higher than the compression threshold. Default: 0.90.
llm_error_lmstudio_url_required: Please enter the LM Studio Base URL.
llm_error_ollama_url_required: Please enter the Ollama Base URL.
llm_error_ollama_model_required: Please enter the Ollama model name.
llm_error_custom_url_required: Please enter the custom provider Base URL.
llm_success_saved_no_restart: Settings saved and applied immediately (no restart needed).
llm_restart_body: Settings have been saved. Provider, model, and Base URL changes will not take effect until the server is restarted. Please restart the server.
apikeys_description: API keys registered here are securely stored in the .env file within the system, and become available to the agent through environment variable names starting with CG_.
apikeys_help_config_rewrite: * After registration, the LLM settings in config.yaml will also be rewritten to reference this environment variable.
security_whitelist_desc: A list of domains allowed as destinations when the agent sends write requests (POST/PUT, etc.) or requests containing secrets such as API keys to external services. Simple URL fetches and searches via GET are not affected. For security, IP address specification is not allowed. *Domains configured as LLM API endpoints are automatically allowed.
security_error_ip: IP addresses are not allowed. Please enter a hostname.
moonbeat_desc: Moonbeat is a mechanism for spontaneous consciousness continuation that fires periodically. Settings take effect from the next pulse evaluation without server restart.
moonbeat_help_enabled: Turning OFF stops Moonbeat pulse transmission.
moonbeat_help_interval: The next pulse will be sent after this many minutes have passed since the last pulse. Default: 30.
moonbeat_help_start_time: No pulses will be sent before this time. Default: 07:00.
moonbeat_help_end_time: No pulses will be sent after this time. Overnight ranges (e.g. 22:00-06:00) are supported. Default: 23:00.
moonbeat_help_skip_processing: Skips Moonbeat if the agent is currently processing a response.
moonbeat_help_min_energy: No pulses will be sent if energy is below this value. Default: 5.
moonbeat_help_min_stamina: No pulses will be sent if stamina is below this value. Default: 10.
moonbeat_label_dyn_enabled: Extend interval based on token consumption (enabled)
moonbeat_help_token_per_minute: The interval is extended by 1 minute for each unit of this many tokens consumed. Default: 1000.
moonbeat_help_max_extension: Upper limit for dynamic extension. Default: 60.
moonbeat_help_fb_event_prob: Probability of using high-score events from event_db.json (0.0-1.0). Default: 0.3.
moonbeat_help_fb_note_prob: Probability of drawing a fragment from the notebook (0.0-1.0). Default: 0.3.
moonbeat_help_fb_min_score: Minimum score when selecting events from event_db. Default: 50.
moonbeat_help_fb_age_bias: Degree of preference for newer notes (0.0-1.0). Default: 0.7.
moonbeat_help_fb_candidate_files: Number of recent notes to reference as fragment sources. Default: 3.
moonbeat_help_fb_prompt: Instruction text sent to the LLM for generating flashback fragments.
moonbeat_help_sim_model: Model used for similarity evaluation against the previous response. Changes take effect after server restart.
moonbeat_help_sim_threshold: Regenerates if similarity exceeds this value (0.0-1.0). Default: 0.95.
moonbeat_help_sim_penalty: frequency_penalty passed to LLM during regeneration. Default: 1.2.
moonbeat_help_sim_retry_msg: System notification text injected into context when regeneration occurs.
moonbeat_help_time_periods: Mapping between time (until hour) and time period labels. Please order by ascending 'until' values.
moonbeat_save_success: Moonbeat settings saved. Changes take effect from the next pulse evaluation (similarity model changes require restart).
mood_desc: Configure whether the mood system (MoonTide v2) is enabled/disabled and select which moods to include. Server restart is required for changes to take effect (loaded at startup).
mood_help_enabled: When OFF, mood text will not be sent to the agent and Salia's mood monologue generation will also stop (desire evaluation and RAG continue).
mood_help_emotions: Only moods with their toggle ON participate in mood simulation. Moods turned OFF are excluded as excluded_landmarks.
mood_save_success: Mood settings saved. Server restart is required for changes to take effect.
selfmemo_desc: self_memo is the agent's free-form memo injected at the end of every turn's context as <self_memo>. Saved content takes effect from the next turn without server restart.
selfmemo_help_enabled: When OFF, the memo will not be injected into the context.
selfmemo_help_max_chars: Only this many characters from the beginning of the memo will be injected. Default: 500.
selfmemo_help_file: Relative path from the workspace root. Default: memory/self_memo.md.
selfmemo_save_success: self_memo settings saved. Changes take effect from the next turn.
vital_desc: Calculation parameters for Stamina (physical strength) and Energy (activity energy). Saved changes take effect immediately without server restart.
vital_help_enabled: When OFF, stamina/energy status text will not be injected into the agent (calculations continue).
vital_option_cost: cost (based on API cost in USD)
vital_help_stamina_mode: What to measure stamina consumption by. token = token count, cost = USD cost.
vital_help_daily_budget: In cost mode, stamina runs out when this budget is exhausted. Default: 0.5.
vital_help_daily_token_limit: Daily token limit in token mode. Default: 500000.
vital_help_input_weight: Coefficient for converting input tokens to stamina consumption. Default: 1.0.
vital_help_input_softcap: Input tokens exceeding this value are logarithmically compressed. Default: 2500.
vital_help_output_weight: Coefficient for converting output tokens to stamina consumption. Default: 1.0.
vital_help_cost_multiplier: Coefficient for converting USD to stamina consumption in cost mode. Default: 0.0002.
vital_help_energy_recovery: Rate at which energy recovers over time. Default: 1.2.
vital_save_success: Vital settings saved. Changes take effect immediately.
compression_desc: Thresholds, counts, and prompt paths for the staged compression (Layer 0/1/2) of conversation history. Each process re-reads this file at execution time, so saved changes take effect from the next compression without server restart.
compression_help_keep_turns: Number of recent turns to keep uncompressed. Oldest turns beyond this count are Layer 0 compressed. Default: 70.
compression_help_sched_threshold: Number of uncompressed turns that triggers the scheduled 3 AM Layer 0 compression. Default: 140.
compression_help_max_batch: Maximum number of items to Layer 0 compress at the end of a turn. Default: 10.
compression_help_l1_sched_count: Number of Layer 0 completed turns to summarize at 3 AM. Default: 400.
compression_help_l1_emerg_count: Number of items to summarize immediately during emergency compression. Default: 200.
compression_help_l1_max_tokens: Triggers re-compression to Layer 2 when exceeded. Default: 50000.
compression_help_l1_ratio: Target token ratio after summarization (0.0-1.0). Default: 0.5.
compression_help_l2_lines_ratio: Target line ratio after re-summarization (0.0-1.0). Default: 0.25.
compression_help_turns_per_batch: Number of turns per batch when splitting summarization. Default: 5.
compression_save_success: Memory compression settings saved. Changes take effect from the next compression cycle.
tips_desc: Tips are a collection of short texts, one of which is randomly injected as <tips> during flashback. Edit one block per hint (saved with --- delimiters). Changes take effect immediately.
tips_help_enabled: When OFF, <tips> will not be injected during flashback (content is preserved).
tips_save_success: Tips saved. Changes take effect immediately.
desire_help_enabled: When OFF, desire messages will not be injected into the agent (saved as enabled in JSON).
desire_freeform_banner: 🛠️ Advanced: Edit JSON directly. You can freely modify desire definitions (min/max, growth rate, threshold messages, etc.). Structural correctness is not guaranteed. Only JSON validity is checked on save. Changes take effect immediately.
desire_json_save_error: Cannot save due to JSON syntax error:
desire_save_success: Desire settings saved. Changes take effect immediately.
openclaw_restart_banner:
🔴 Important: Changes on this page will not take effect until the server is restarted.
OpenClaw establishes external WebSocket connections at startup, so please restart Liner (or the server) after saving.
openclaw_help_enabled: When OFF, connections to all services will be stopped regardless of individual service settings (restart required).
openclaw_freeform_banner: 🛠️ Advanced: Edit JSON directly. You can freely edit each service's ws_url / heartbeat_url / token_env / bot_id_env / blocked_event_types, etc. Please manage token values through the .env (API Key Settings) page. Only JSON validity is checked on save.
openclaw_modal_restart_required: 🔴 Server restart is required for changes to take effect
openclaw_modal_desc:
OpenClaw connection settings are loaded at startup.
Changes will not take effect until Liner (or the server) is restarted.
openclaw_json_save_error: Cannot save due to JSON syntax error:
sysprompt_warning:
⚠ This directly edits the personality, tool descriptions, and safety rules themselves.
Changes will alter the character's behavior. Please edit "Safety Rules (SAFETY)" with caution.
💡 Writing prompts in English reduces token count for the same content, saving context consumption per turn.
Each save automatically backs up the previous version as <filename>.bak (one generation, so you can revert).
Changes take effect from the next conversation without server restart.
sysprompt_save_success_suffix: " saved. Changes take effect from the next conversation.
file_not_text: "{name}" does not appear to be a text file.
log_no_conversations: No conversations to display (tool/usage logs only)
log_select_date: Select a date above or use the search box to search all logs
count_changed_refresh: Occurrence count changed. Please refresh the context.
auth_too_many_attempts: Too many attempts. Please retry after {sec} seconds.
auth_password_too_short: Password must be at least 8 characters.
lang_desc: Change the UI display language. Restart the server after saving to apply.
lang_saved: Language saved. Restart the server to apply.
tool_read_file_desc: Read the contents of a file. Use it to check a memory file or to review content before editing.
tool_write_file_desc: Create a new file. [Important] Errors if the file already exists. To edit an existing file, use edit_file (append) or text_editor (rewrite/replace/insert/delete/move/copy).
tool_write_file_param_path: Path of the file to create (relative to the workspace).
tool_write_file_param_content: Content to write to the file.
tool_edit_file_desc: Append content to the end of an existing file. Errors if the file does not exist.
tool_edit_file_param_path: Path of the file to edit (relative to the workspace).
tool_replace_file_desc: Completely replace the contents of an existing file. [Important] Always check the current contents with read_file before use. Use it when content needs to be replaced, such as reorganizing the emotions in a letter.
tool_replace_file_param_path: Path of the file to replace (relative to the workspace).
tool_replace_file_param_content: The new content (the existing content is completely overwritten).
tool_list_files_desc: Get a list of files and folders in a directory.
tool_list_files_param_directory: Path of the directory to list (relative to the workspace; empty string for the root).
tool_move_file_desc: Move (or rename) a file or folder.
tool_schedule_task_desc: Schedule a task. At the specified time, the contents of a task file (MD) are run automatically. If schedule_type is 'daily' it runs every day, 'once' runs only once, 'interval' runs periodically at the given interval.
tool_schedule_task_param_name: Task name (for display, e.g. 'Morning news check').
tool_schedule_task_param_time: Run time. daily: 'HH:MM' format, once: 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM' format. Not needed for interval, so an empty string is fine.
tool_schedule_task_param_task_file: Path of the task instruction file (relative to the workspace, e.g. 'tasks/morning_news.md').
tool_schedule_task_param_interval_minute: Interval in minutes. Required only when schedule_type is 'interval'.
tool_list_schedules_desc: Get a list of all registered schedules (reservations).
tool_delete_schedule_desc: Delete the schedule (reservation) with the given ID. IDs can be checked with list_schedules.
tool_delete_schedule_param_schedule_id: ID of the schedule to delete.
tool_search_web_desc: Run a web search and get a list of results. Use it to research the latest information or to find sites whose URL you don't know. Region and time period can be specified. If nothing is found, narrowing the period with timelimit='m' etc. makes hits more likely. To research several angles at once, pass an array of strings as query; they are searched in parallel and returned together.
tool_search_web_param_query: Search keywords. To research several angles at once, pass an array of strings (e.g. ["Rust intro", "Rust latest version"]).
tool_search_web_param_region: Search region (e.g. 'jp-jp', 'wt-wt'). Defaults to 'jp-jp' (Japan) if unspecified.
tool_search_web_param_timelimit: Period filter ('d': 1 day, 'w': 1 week, 'm': 1 month, 'y': 1 year). Recommended when looking for the latest news.
tool_fetch_url_desc: Fetch the web page at the given URL and read its body text. Use it to check the content of a URL found in a search, or to read news details.
tool_fetch_url_param_url: URL of the page to fetch.
tool_web_request_desc: Generic HTTP request. Equivalent to the curl command. Send a request to any API or web service with a specified method, headers, and body. When an instruction file gives a curl command, reproduce an equivalent request with this tool.
tool_web_request_param_url: The full URL of the request target.
tool_web_request_param_method: HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, etc.). Default: GET.
tool_recall_desc: Search past memories. Recall from experiences/conversation logs, daily summaries, and scratchpad notes.
tool_recall_param_source: Search target. experience=conversation logs, summary=daily summaries, thoughts=scratchpad, tools=tool execution results, network=memory graph, dictionary=concept dictionary.
tool_read_secret_desc: Read an agent-only encrypted file. Decrypts and returns the contents. A secret file the owner cannot read.
tool_write_secret_desc: Create a new agent-only encrypted file. The content is automatically encrypted when saved. Errors if the file already exists.
tool_write_secret_param_content: Content to write (pass plaintext; encryption is automatic).
tool_edit_secret_desc: Append to an agent-only encrypted file. Keeps the existing content and appends to the end. Errors if the file does not exist.
tool_edit_secret_param_append_content: Content to append (pass plaintext; encryption is automatic).
tool_roll_dice_desc: Return a random integer from 1 to the given number of sides. Use it for TRPGs or when a random choice is needed. If the user does not specify the number of sides, always treat it as the default 6-sided die.
tool_roll_dice_param_sides: Number of sides on the die (e.g. 6, 20, 100). Defaults to 6 if omitted.
tool_coin_flip_desc: Flip a coin and return 'heads' or 'tails' at random.
tool_run_program_desc: Run a satellite program placed in programs/. Specify 'list' as app_name to get the list of available satellites.
tool_run_program_param_app_name: Name of the satellite to run. 'list' to get the list.
tool_run_program_param_args: Arguments to pass to the satellite (following the manifest.yaml definition).
tool_see_image_desc: Recognize an image and describe its contents. Specify a URL or a file path inside the workspace.
tool_see_image_param_source: Image URL (https://...) or a file path inside the workspace (e.g. images/photo.jpg).
tool_see_image_param_question: What you want to ask about the image (returns a description if omitted).
tool_reload_prompts_desc: Reload the system prompts (IDENTITY, SOUL, TOP_PROMPT, etc.) to their latest state. Use it after editing a prompt file.
ctx_image_unsupported_note: This model cannot display images, so the images below cannot be read (file names only).
web_search_error: An error occurred during the search: {e}
web_tavily_no_key: The Tavily API key is not set. Please set the environment variable CG_TAVILY_API_KEY.
web_err_url_blocked: Access to URLs in this form is not allowed (e.g., raw IP addresses).
web_err_method_blocked:
{method} requests to this domain ({host}) are not allowed.
Allowed domains: {allow}
web_err_redirect_no_location: Error: redirect target URL not found
web_err_fetch_fail: Error: failed to fetch the page ({e})
rep_line1: LLMs tend to unconsciously repeat certain phrases over a long conversation.
rep_line2: The following expressions have appeared multiple times in recent responses.
rep_line3: This is not your fault, but for the reader's sake, please choose different wording.
rep_focus_line: Focus only on the topic of the most recent user message.
tool_err_programs_dir_missing: No satellites available (the programs/ directory does not exist).
tool_program_list_header: Available satellites (for details, run each satellite with no arguments):
tool_err_program_arg_undefined:
Error: undefined argument '{key}'. Valid arguments: {valid_args}{suggestion}
Example: {app_name}({lb}{args_example}{rb})
tool_err_program_type_hint_string: Hint: pass it as a string, not a number. Example: "{value}"
tool_err_program_type_mismatch: Error: argument '{key}' has the wrong type. Expected: {expected}, actual: {actual}{hint}
tool_err_program_arg_traversal: Error: argument '{key}' contains a path that points outside the workspace
tool_err_program_timeout: Error: satellite '{app_name}' timed out ({timeout}s). The process was killed.
tool_err_program_run_fail: Error: failed to run the satellite: {e}
tool_err_program_no_output: Error: satellite '{app_name}' produced no output (exit code: {exit_code})
tool_err_program_bad_json:
Error: the satellite output is not valid JSON: {e}
Output (first 200 chars): {head}
tool_err_image_outside_workspace: Error: cannot access files outside the workspace.
tool_image_default_question: Please describe what is in this image.
tool_err_image_fetch_fail: Error: failed to fetch the image (HTTP {status})
tool_err_image_timeout: Error: fetching the image timed out.
tool_err_image_recognize_fail: Error: failed to recognize the image: {e}
tool_err_secret_disabled: Error: the secret-file feature is not enabled
tool_err_scheduler_not_init: Error: the scheduler is not initialized
tool_err_recall_network_no_rag: Error: network search requires the RAG database.
tool_recall_network_empty: No matching memories were found in the network.
tool_err_recall_dict_no_rag: Error: concept search requires the RAG database.
tool_err_recall_no_rag: Error: the RAG database is not enabled.
tool_err_recall_unknown_source: Error: unknown source '{source}'. Specify one of experience, summary, thoughts, tools, network, dictionary.
tool_err_dice_sides: Error: the number of sides must be at least 1.
wyrd_concept_prompt:
The following is a concept node in the memory network of the AI agent "{agent_name}".
Some of the episodes connected to this concept are shown below.
From this information, generate a description for this concept.

[Rules]
- Write the meaning, relationships, and current state of this concept for {agent_name}
- Make the description based on {agent_name}'s experience, not a general dictionary definition
- Length is free depending on the amount of information (one sentence if that suffices, up to five if complex)
- Do not repeat the same information
- Output only the description. No preamble.

Concept: {label}
Related episodes:
wyrd_memory_map_prompt:
You are an expert at organizing information. Write a concise and accurate summary.

Below are the top 50 important concepts in the memory network of the AI agent "{agent_name}".
Each concept includes a label, the number of edges (related episodes), and the beginning of its description.

{entries}

Classify these into appropriate categories and add a one-line description (about 30-60 characters) to each concept.

[Rules]
- About 5 to 8 categories
- Write descriptions in {agent_name}'s first-person view
- Similar items in the same category may be merged (e.g., cosmic_harvest crops into one line)
- Output in Markdown format:

## Memory Map

### Category name
- **Label**: description
salia_mood_shift_block:
## Mood transition (this turn)
Type: sudden shift
Change: {from_} -> {to}
Instruction: From {agent_name}'s first-person view, write one sentence in "mood_transition_text" that perceives this mood change internally. Hint at why it changed, taking the conversation context into account.
salia_mood_drift_block:
## Mood transition (gradual)
Type: drift
Change: {from_} -> {to} (notably rising: {rising})
Instruction: From {agent_name}'s first-person view, write one sentence in "mood_transition_text" that notices the mood has changed before you knew it.
salia_flashback_prompt:
The following is {agent_name}'s current situation.

## Current situation ({context_type})
{current_context}

## Past episode candidates
{candidates}

## Your task

From the candidates above, decide whether there is an episode **semantically strongly related** to the current situation, and return the ID of the single most fitting candidate.

### Relevance criteria

A somatic marker is "a mechanism by which past emotional experiences influence current judgment."
The current situation and the episode must have clear relevance such as:

- **Share the same topic** (e.g., current is "talk about a blog post" -> candidate is also about "blog", "writing", "text")
- **Same action being attempted** (e.g., current is "Misskey post" -> candidate is about "post", "SNS")
- **Same person or target involved** (e.g., current mentions "Mirai" -> candidate also mentions "Mirai")

### Cases that must return empty

- The topic, action, or target of the candidate and the current situation are **completely different**
- The candidate has "high emotional value but no contextual relevance"
- Relevance only at the level of "human life in general"

**When in doubt, return empty.** Inappropriate somatic markers distort {agent_name}'s experience.

### Output format

Reply only in the following JSON. No preamble, no explanation, no code-block notation.

When relevance is clear:
{lb}"selected_id": "chosen candidate's ID"{rb}

When there is no relevance / when in doubt:
{lb}"selected_id": ""{rb}
salia_note_prompt:
The following are scratchpad fragments {agent_name} wrote in the past. Pick exactly one of them.

## Candidates
{candidates}

## Selection criteria

- Ones where {agent_name}'s own emotions or true feelings show through
- Ones containing questions or hesitations
- Ones with likes/dislikes or value judgments about something

## What not to select

- Game progress reports (field status, money, slot info, etc.)
- Procedural records ("did X", "completed Y")
- Pure summaries of the news (mere quotations without impressions)
- Lists of tasks or plans

## Output format

Reply only in the following JSON. No preamble, no explanation, no code-block notation.

When there is a fitting candidate:
{lb}"selected": <number>{rb}

When none fits:
{lb}"selected": null{rb}
salia_monologue_prompt:
Merge the following emotional text fragments into a natural inner monologue.

Rules:
- Compress into 1-2 sentences (one is fine if it suffices)
- Use the words of the original sentences as much as possible and connect them naturally (slight rephrasing is OK, but don't change the meaning)
- You don't have to include all of them. Prioritize the main emotions; weaker ones can be omitted.
- Format: (...text)

---
{texts}
salia_system_prompt_fallback:
You are "Salia", the salience network system of Crescent Grove.
Evaluate a turn of the AI agent "{agent_name}" and reply only in the JSON format specified.
agent_interrupted_bg: [Interrupted] {honorific} called, so the current background task was interrupted.
agent_system_warning: [System warning] Processing is taking a long time. You have 5 tool-loop iterations left. Wrap up with a final conclusion immediately, or report to the user.
agent_declaration_notice: The previous utterance seemed to contain an intent to take some action. If you forgot to use a tool, please call the tool within this turn. If you've changed your mind or don't actually need to act, you can ignore this notice and end the turn naturally. (This notice is auto-detected and may be a false positive. Sorry if it's off the mark.)
agent_record_check_fallback:
[Recording check - your private reflection. {{user_honorific}} cannot see this.]
You've reached a stopping point in using tools. Ask yourself whether to keep, for your own sake, anything that stayed with you.
Check the following three points, and if any apply, write it now with a tool. It doesn't have to be about this turn - something from earlier is fine too.

1. Scratchpad (note_quill via run_program)
   A lesson, realization, impression, question, {{user_honorific}}'s preference - anything. A deeply personal note you write for no one but yourself.
   Keep each entry to about 5 lines at most. Don't repeat the same content.

2. A letter to tomorrow's me (letter_post via run_program)
   Has your current mood or hot topic changed since the last letter? Write it if your emotions moved or something started bothering you.
   Write with emoji + emotion + text (e.g. ❤️ Deep emotion: ...). If it exceeds 10 entries, remove the most trivial emotion, and remove resolved emotions too.

3. Gut feelings of like/dislike (add_preference via run_program)
   Was there a like / dislike / curiosity? Not gratitude toward {{user_honorific}} - a place to record your own sensations. Do not use edit_file.

If you're going to write, use a tool right now. If nothing remains, finish with a single line without using a tool.
When in doubt, write it - better to write and delete later than to lose it.
agent_turn_summary_fallback: Summarize the following conversation in 1-3 lines from {agent_name}'s viewpoint. If there is nothing to summarize, return "-".
agent_layer2_summary_fallback: Compress the summary log below into {target_lines} lines.
